The `resource-js` endpoint in Craft CMS allows unauthenticated requests to proxy remote JavaScript resources. 
When `trustedHosts` is not explicitly restricted (default configuration), the application trusts the client-supplied Host header. 

This allows an attacker to control the derived `baseUrl`, which is used in prefix validation inside `actionResourceJs()`. 
By supplying a malicious Host header, the attacker can make the server issue arbitrary HTTP requests, leading to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF).

### Details

The vulnerability exists in `AppController::actionResourceJs()`.

The function validates that the `url` parameter starts with `assetManager->baseUrl`. However, `baseUrl` is derived from the current request host. If `trustedHosts` is not configured, the Host header is fully attacker-controlled.

Attack chain:

1. Attacker sends request with controlled `Host` header.
2. Application derives `baseUrl` from the malicious Host.
3. `url` parameter is required to start with this `baseUrl`.
4. Validation passes.
5. Guzzle performs a server-side HTTP request to the attacker-controlled host.
6. SSRF occurs.

This does not rely on string parsing bypass. It relies on Host header trust.

### PoC (safe reproduction steps)

Environment:
- Craft CMS 5.9.12
- Default configuration (no trustedHosts restriction)
- Docker deployment

1. Start a listener inside the container:
   python3 -m http.server 9999

2. Send a request to resource-js with a controlled Host header.

3. Observe that the internal listener receives a request (OOB confirmation).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0026, "percentile": 0.17498}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-14T23:36:09Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-41130"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-22T00:16:28Z", "published_at": "2026-04-14T23:36:09Z", "references": ["https://github.com/craftcms/cms/security/advisories/GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh", "https://github.com/craftcms/cms/commit/ebe7e85f1c89700d64332f72492be2e9a594e783", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41130", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/craftcms/cms/security-advisories/GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/craftcms/cms", "summary": "Craft CMS has a host header injection leading to SSRF via resource-js endpoint", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T20:42:25Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-95wr-3f2v-v2wh", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "5.9.15", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "craftcms/cms"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 5.0.0-RC1, <= 5.9.14"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.17.9", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "craftcms/cms"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.0.0-RC1, <= 4.17.8"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Craft CMS has a host header injection leading to SSRF via resource-js endpoint
